verdict.answer_first: B is slightly better: sentence 1 says the result, "find rows without scanning every row"; A says "speed up lookups" but buries the add-index rule until the end.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more clearly with "start with no indexes beyond your keys... then index deliberately"; A is more of a checklist, "Columns in WHERE... Join columns... ORDER BY".
verdict.fabrication: none; B's "~20 comparisons and 1,000,000" is a simplified B-tree/log(N) illustration, not an unsupported business metric.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives a trigger and verification loop: "Run EXPLAIN... sequential/full table scan... add an index... confirm" plus specific traps like "LOWER(email)" and "LIKE '%smith'".
